tasks.title: slugify() leaves doubled and trailing separators
Gravity solver uses the wrong force law away from unit radius

runs.task_title: slugify() leaves doubled and trailing separators
runs.bug_report:
Our CMS builds URL slugs with slugify() in solution.py. Editors are reporting
broken links: titles that contain punctuation or extra spacing come out with
doubled hyphens, and some slugs begin or end with a hyphen.

For example "Hello,  World!" should become "hello-world" but does not.

Fix slugify() so that any run of non-alphanumeric characters becomes exactly
one hyphen, and the result never starts or ends with a hyphen.
runs.patch:
--- a/solution.py
+++ b/solution.py
@@ -9,4 +9,4 @@
     normalized = unicodedata.normalize("NFKD", title)
     ascii_only = normalized.encode("ascii", "ignore").decode("ascii")
     lowered = ascii_only.lower()
-    return re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]", "-", lowered)
+    return re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "-", lowered).strip("-")

runs.task_title: Gravity solver uses the wrong force law away from unit radius
runs.bug_report:
The orbital viewer looks correct on screen but bodies drift out of their
orbits over time, and the effect gets worse the further out the body starts.

A QA engineer noticed that acceleration_magnitude(3.0) returns 0.333 when it
should return 0.111 for a unit gravitational parameter. Orbits starting at
radius 1.0 look fine, which is why this was missed for so long.

Fix the physics in solution.py so acceleration follows an inverse-square law
at every radius.
runs.patch:
--- a/solution.py
+++ b/solution.py
@@ -11,8 +11,9 @@
 
 def acceleration(x: float, y: float, gm: float = GM) -> tuple[float, float]:
     """Gravitational acceleration vector at position (x, y)."""
-    r_squared = x * x + y * y
-    return (-gm * x / r_squared, -gm * y / r_squared)
+    r = math.hypot(x, y)
+    r_cubed = r * r * r
+    return (-gm * x / r_cubed, -gm * y / r_cubed)
 
 
 def acceleration_magnitude(r: float, gm: float = GM) -> float:
runs.error: ServerError: 503 UNAVAILABLE. {'error': {'code': 503, 'message': 'This model is currently experiencing high demand. Spikes in demand are usually temporary. Please try again later.', 'status': 'UNAVAILABLE'}}
